rules.description: Applicable model-family or contaminant-family governance snapshots must be present on the dossier.
All source ids referenced by the dossier must resolve to governed source records.
Dossier must include all required pinned-resource fingerprints for governance-aware review.
Governance-required disclaimers must remain present in dossier-facing notes or limitations.
Scientific blockers, unresolved error-quality flags, and open blocking signoff actions must not remain unresolved on the dossier.
Target readiness jurisdiction must align with the dossier governance and interpretation context.
Historical data-period or source-currency limitations must remain explicit and block submission-candidate packaging until reviewed.
Legal-limit support posture must remain explicit on the dossier, and submission-candidate review is downgraded when support is partial or missing.
Deprecated governance states must not silently pass readiness review.
Model-family or contaminant-family governance may block submission-candidate use or downgrade internal review.
Normative source document statuses must be compatible with the target readiness profile.
Normative sources explicitly blocked from submission use fail submission-candidate review.
Technical-report dependencies require review before submission-candidate packaging.
Active errata may downgrade or fail readiness depending on blocking status and profile.
Consultation-oriented review allows compatible model or contaminant-family governance states.
Consultation-oriented review requires explicit consultation wording on the dossier.
Consultation-oriented review downgrades dossiers with unresolved governance sources.
